Patch Set 6:

(1 comment)

Patch Set 6:

Code-wise looks good to me.

Patch Set 6: Code-Review+1

> (1 comment)

do we clear the FLR state when deleting the second to last mirror?

Patch Set 6:

> > (1 comment)
 > 
 > do we clear the FLR state when deleting the second to last mirror?

yes, after the split the state was cleared to 0.

Patch Set 6:

I think there is a problem to drop the mirror ID from the file completely if the second-to-last mirror is removed.  The mirror ID also makes up part of the component ID, and changing that while the file is referenced by any external application would make it hard to identify the remaining component. 

For example, if there is a user working on a file with "mirror #1" and "mirror #2", and then removes "mirror #1" from the file. If they want to add a new mirror there may be confusion about which mirror to use.
Patch Set 6: Code-Review+2
Change has been successfully cherry-picked as 325e23899aa38de32ec00b19ed675bcc64c6e5c8 by Oleg Drokin

parents.subject: LU-11238 lod: refine obj avoid collect for FLR

commit.subject: LU-11212 lod: preserve mirror ID on mirror extension
commit.message:
LU-11212 lod: preserve mirror ID on mirror extension

When merging/expanding existing mirrors of a FLR file, we need keep
its existing mirror's mirror ID.

Signed-off-by: Bobi Jam <bobijam.xu@intel.com>
Change-Id: If139076c37c33bb1a330e1a5e997f8f56015fd9a
Reviewed-on: https://review.whamcloud.com/32938
Tested-by: Jenkins
Tested-by: Maloo <hpdd-maloo@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <adilger@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Jian Yu <yujian@whamcloud.com>
Reviewed-by: Jinshan Xiong <jinshan.xiong@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <green@whamcloud.com>
